item.title: Fully Funded Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028 for Health Equity Leaders in Southeast Asia and China
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Appeynarh
item.description:
The Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028, also known as the Atlantic Fellows for Health Equity in Southeast Asia (AFHESEA), is a fully funded and immersive leadership development programme created to build a new generation of leaders committed to advancing health equity across Southeast Asia and China. The Fellowship is an initiative of the CMB Foundation and […]

The post Fully Funded Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028 for Health Equity Leaders in Southeast Asia and China appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028, also known as the Atlantic Fellows for Health Equity in Southeast Asia (AFHESEA), is a fully funded and immersive leadership development programme created to build a new generation of leaders committed to advancing health equity across Southeast Asia and China.

The Fellowship is an initiative of the CMB Foundation and forms part of the prestigious global Atlantic Fellows network, a worldwide community of leaders working across sectors and regions to address inequality and create lasting social change.

The programme supports professionals who are passionate about tackling the deeper causes of health inequity, including social, economic, political, and structural barriers that prevent communities from achieving equal opportunities for health and wellbeing.

Applications for the 2027–2028 Equity Initiative Fellowship open on June 15.

About the Equity Initiative Fellowship

The Equity Initiative Fellowship provides selected Fellows with a comprehensive leadership journey that combines intensive in-person learning experiences with continuous online engagement.

The Fellowship is designed around the belief that improving health outcomes requires more than medical interventions. Health equity is influenced by broader social conditions, including:

  • Access to quality education.
  • Employment opportunities.
  • Housing conditions.
  • Food security and nutrition.
  • Environmental conditions.
  • Social inclusion.
  • Political and economic systems.
  • Access to affordable and quality healthcare services.

Through the Fellowship, participants explore how these factors shape health outcomes and learn how leaders can influence policies, systems, and communities to create fairer and healthier societies.

The programme develops leaders who can identify inequities, understand their root causes, design solutions, and collaborate across sectors to create meaningful change.

Fully Funded Learning Experience for Emerging Health Equity Leaders

The Equity Initiative Fellowship provides a fully funded learning experience that includes:

  • Six weeks of in-person learning activities throughout the Induction Year.
  • Online learning sessions and engagement activities.
  • Leadership development opportunities.
  • Exposure to global and regional health equity experts.
  • Peer networking with other transformative leaders.
  • Practical learning experiences focused on solving real-world challenges.

The Fellowship follows a carefully designed learning journey that begins within the Southeast Asia and China region, expands to a global perspective, and then returns to regional applications.

This approach allows Fellows to:

  • Understand local and regional health challenges.
  • Learn from international experiences and approaches.
  • Apply global knowledge to their own communities.
  • Build long-term relationships with other leaders working toward social change.

The “Know, Do, Be” Framework

The Equity Initiative Fellowship curriculum is built around the “Know, Do, Be” leadership framework, which focuses on three essential areas of leadership development.

1. Know: Building Knowledge and Understanding

Fellows develop a strong understanding of:

  • Health equity concepts.
  • Social determinants of health.
  • Systems thinking.
  • Structural causes of inequality.
  • The relationship between policy, financing, healthcare delivery, and social conditions.

Participants examine why certain communities experience poorer health outcomes and how broader societal systems contribute to these inequalities.

2. Do: Applying Knowledge Through Action

The Fellowship provides practical opportunities for Fellows to apply their knowledge through:

  • Leadership tools.
  • Problem-solving exercises.
  • Experiential learning activities.
  • Innovation laboratories.
  • Collaborative projects.

Fellows learn how to translate ideas into practical solutions that address real health equity challenges.

3. Be: Developing Purpose-Driven Leadership

The programme encourages Fellows to develop a leadership mindset focused on:

  • Reflection.
  • Ethical decision-making.
  • Effective communication.
  • Public leadership.
  • Commitment to social justice.

Participants are encouraged to become leaders who do not simply understand inequality but actively work toward transforming systems that create unequal outcomes.

Why Health Equity and Leadership Matter

Health equity is one of the most important global challenges facing societies today.

While healthcare access is a major part of health outcomes, many inequalities originate from factors outside hospitals and clinics.

Social determinants such as employment, education, housing, environmental conditions, and political systems significantly influence whether individuals and communities can achieve good health.

Across Southeast Asia and China, rapid economic growth has created opportunities but has also highlighted significant inequalities affecting vulnerable groups.

Some major health equity challenges include:

  • Unequal access to healthcare services.
  • Barriers to primary healthcare.
  • Health financing inequalities.
  • Healthcare workforce shortages in disadvantaged communities.
  • Environmental degradation.
  • The impact of armed conflict and humanitarian crises.
  • Challenges affecting women and children.
  • Healthcare barriers faced by migrants and refugees.
  • Inequalities affecting ethnic minorities and economically disadvantaged populations.

The Equity Initiative believes that leadership plays a critical role in addressing these challenges.

Leaders can either maintain systems that create inequality or become catalysts for positive transformation.

Through this Fellowship, participants develop the knowledge, skills, and networks required to influence policies, strengthen communities, and create sustainable improvements in health equity.

Fellowship Induction Year: Learning Structure and Activities

During the Induction Year, Fellows participate in a combination of:

  • In-person learning events.
  • Online learning activities.
  • Leadership discussions.
  • Expert conversations.
  • Collaborative projects.
  • Peer-to-peer exchanges.

The programme requires Fellows to actively participate in all mandatory learning activities.

Participants are expected to complete:

  • Approximately six weeks of in-person Fellowship learning events throughout the Induction Year.
  • Regular online learning activities estimated at approximately one hour per week.

The Fellowship creates a collaborative environment where participants learn from different cultures, professional backgrounds, and experiences.

For more information visit here

Learning Objectives of the Equity Initiative Fellowship

Throughout the Induction Year, Fellows will:

Deepen Their Understanding of Health Inequality

Participants will explore:

  • The social determinants of health.
  • Structural barriers affecting health outcomes.
  • The relationship between inequality and wellbeing.
  • Systems that contribute to unequal access to services.

Develop Advanced Leadership Skills

Fellows will strengthen their ability to:

  • Lead social change initiatives.
  • Influence decision-makers.
  • Collaborate across sectors.
  • Build sustainable solutions.
  • Manage complex challenges affecting communities.

Engage With Experts and Global Change-Makers

Participants will have opportunities to interact with:

  • Health equity specialists.
  • Policy experts.
  • Researchers.
  • Public leaders.
  • Community advocates.
  • Fellow innovators.

These engagements encourage knowledge exchange and cross-sector collaboration.

Create Innovative Solutions for Health Equity Challenges

Fellows will explore strategies to:

  • Remove systemic barriers.
  • Improve access to essential services.
  • Promote fairness in health systems.
  • Support disadvantaged populations.
  • Develop community-driven solutions.

Build a Regional Network of Leaders

The Fellowship connects participants with a diverse community of leaders committed to:

  • Social justice.
  • Health equity.
  • Public leadership.
  • Community empowerment.

This network continues beyond the programme through the wider Atlantic Fellows community.

Group Project Development Opportunity

During the Fellowship, participants will have the opportunity to design and develop a group project focused on advancing health equity within their communities.

The project component allows Fellows to:

  • Apply lessons learned during the programme.
  • Work collaboratively with other Fellows.
  • Address practical challenges affecting communities.
  • Create solutions that can continue beyond the Fellowship period.

This practical approach ensures that learning leads to measurable impact.

Becoming Part of the Global Atlantic Fellows Network

After successfully completing the Induction Year, Equity Initiative Fellows become members of the global Atlantic Fellows community.

This lifelong network connects leaders across:

  • Different countries.
  • Professional sectors.
  • Academic disciplines.
  • Social movements.

Members continue collaborating to address inequality, promote justice, and create sustainable social change worldwide.

Benefits of Joining the Equity Initiative Fellowship

Selected Fellows gain access to:

  • A fully funded leadership development experience.
  • Advanced knowledge of health equity and systems change.
  • International and regional networking opportunities.
  • Mentorship and peer support.
  • Exposure to global experts and practitioners.
  • Membership in the Atlantic Fellows global network.
  • Opportunities to develop community-focused solutions.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The Fellowship focuses on applicants from:

  • Southeast Asian countries.
  • China.

The programme is part of the global Atlantic Fellows network, connecting leaders across different regions and sectors who are committed to advancing equity, justice, and social transformation.

For similar opportunities click here.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028 open on June 15.

Applicants should monitor the official programme announcements for the complete application timeline, requirements, and submission procedures.

Conclusion

The Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028 offers a unique opportunity for professionals and leaders committed to creating healthier and more equitable societies across Southeast Asia and China.

By combining leadership development, systems thinking, practical learning, and a global network of changemakers, the Fellowship equips participants with the knowledge and skills needed to address the complex causes of health inequality.

For individuals passionate about public leadership, social justice, healthcare transformation, and community impact, the Equity Initiative Fellowship provides a platform to develop meaningful solutions and become part of a global movement working toward a more equitable future.

Ready to Apply? Click here to Apply

 

The post Fully Funded Equity Initiative Fellowship 2027–2028 for Health Equity Leaders in Southeast Asia and China appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026 for African Organizations: Receive Fundraising Training, Up to $5,000 in Matching Funds, 12 Months of Free Fiscal Sponsorship, and an All-Expenses-Paid Leadership Boot Camp in Kigali
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Appeynarh
item.description:
The New Africa Fund (NAF) has opened applications for the Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026. The ability to secure sustainable funding remains one of the greatest challenges facing nonprofit organizations, charities, social enterprises, and community-based organizations across Africa. While many organizations are delivering transformative solutions to pressing social, economic, environmental, and humanitarian challenges, limited fundraising […]

The post Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026 for African Organizations: Receive Fundraising Training, Up to $5,000 in Matching Funds, 12 Months of Free Fiscal Sponsorship, and an All-Expenses-Paid Leadership Boot Camp in Kigali appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New Africa Fund (NAF) has opened applications for the Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026.

The ability to secure sustainable funding remains one of the greatest challenges facing nonprofit organizations, charities, social enterprises, and community-based organizations across Africa. While many organizations are delivering transformative solutions to pressing social, economic, environmental, and humanitarian challenges, limited fundraising capacity often prevents them from scaling their impact and achieving long-term sustainability.

To address this challenge, the New Africa Fund (NAF) has opened applications for the Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026, a comprehensive multi-stage capacity-building program designed to strengthen fundraising skills among African organizations.

Previously known as the Africa Impact Fundraising Grant (AIFG), the Africa Fundraising Incubator has evolved into a robust program that combines practical fundraising education, institutional support, fiscal sponsorship, matching funds, and leadership development opportunities to help organizations establish sustainable funding pipelines.

Organizations selected for the program will gain access to expert-led fundraising training, participate in a live fundraising challenge with opportunities to receive matching funds of up to $5,000 USD, benefit from 12 months of free fiscal sponsorship, and potentially earn an invitation to an all-expenses-paid, week-long fundraising and leadership boot camp in Kigali, Rwanda.

Application Deadline

Applications close on:

14 August 2026 at 5:00 PM UTC

Late applications may not be considered, so interested organizations are encouraged to prepare their documents and submit their applications before the deadline.

 

About the Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI)

The Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) is a structured fundraising development initiative created by the New Africa Fund (NAF) to strengthen the fundraising capacity of African organizations.

Rather than offering only financial support, AFI focuses on building long-term institutional sustainability by equipping participating organizations with practical fundraising knowledge, donor engagement strategies, and organizational development skills.

The program enables participating organizations to diversify their funding sources, improve donor relationships, strengthen internal systems, and increase their ability to attract sustainable financial support.

By combining education, mentorship, practical fundraising experience, and institutional backing, AFI aims to help African organizations become more resilient and financially sustainable.

 

What the AFI Program Includes

The Africa Fundraising Incubator consists of several carefully designed stages that help organizations move from learning fundraising principles to applying them successfully.

1. Online Fundraising Training

Selected organizations will begin the program with comprehensive online training sessions focused on the core principles of successful fundraising.

Participants will learn practical fundraising techniques and strategies needed to launch and manage successful small-donor fundraising campaigns.

The online sessions are designed to help organizations build strong fundraising foundations while developing sustainable donor engagement strategies.

 

2. 30-Day Fundraising Challenge

After completing the training, participating organizations will immediately apply their newly acquired skills during a live 30-day fundraising campaign.

During this challenge, organizations will:

  • Launch real fundraising campaigns.
  • Engage supporters and donors.
  • Practice donor communication strategies.
  • Build fundraising confidence through practical implementation.

Organizations participating in this stage may receive up to $5,000 USD in matching funds from the New Africa Fund based on program guidelines and performance.

This practical fundraising experience allows participants to move beyond theory and develop real-world fundraising capacity.

 

3. Twelve Months of Free Fiscal Sponsorship

One of the major highlights of the AFI program is the provision of 12 months of free fiscal sponsorship to all participating organizations.

Fiscal sponsorship helps reduce administrative and operational barriers that organizations may face while strengthening their fundraising operations.

The sponsorship may also be extended depending on organizational performance and program considerations.

 

4. All-Expenses-Paid Leadership Boot Camp in Kigali

Top-performing organizations throughout the program will receive invitations to participate in a fully funded, week-long fundraising and leadership training in Kigali, Rwanda.

The intensive in-person boot camp is designed to deepen participants’ fundraising expertise while strengthening organizational leadership and management capacity.

Participants will receive advanced training in areas including:

  • Financial management.
  • Marketing strategies.
  • Organizational communications.
  • Grant writing.
  • Donor research.
  • Donor cultivation.
  • Donor retention.
  • Individual donor fundraising campaigns.
  • Local fundraising strategies.
  • Leadership development.

The boot camp also provides opportunities for networking, collaboration, and learning from fundraising experts and fellow African organizations.

 

Benefits of the Africa Fundraising Incubator

Organizations selected for AFI will enjoy numerous benefits throughout the program.

These include:

  • Comprehensive fundraising capacity-building training.
  • Specialized instruction on individual donor fundraising campaigns.
  • Practical fundraising experience through a live 30-day fundraising challenge.
  • Opportunity to receive up to $5,000 USD in matching funds.
  • Twelve months of free fiscal sponsorship.
  • Possible extension of fiscal sponsorship based on performance.
  • Opportunity to attend an all-expenses-paid leadership and fundraising workshop in Kigali, Rwanda.
  • Advanced fundraising and organizational leadership development.
  • Additional performance-based fundraising opportunities as the program expands.
  • Improved institutional sustainability.
  • Stronger fundraising systems and donor engagement capacity.
  • Access to experienced fundraising professionals and mentors.
  • Opportunities to collaborate with organizations across Africa.

 

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open exclusively to organizations that meet the eligibility requirements established by the New Africa Fund.

To be eligible, applicants must:

  • Be a legally registered organization.
  • Be registered in one of the 54 African Union member states.
  • Be prepared to participate fully in all stages of the program if selected.

Organizations that are not registered in an African Union member state are not eligible to apply.

 

Required Application Documents

Before beginning the application, organizations should prepare the following documents in PDF format.

Applicants will need:

  1. Organization Registration Document.
  2. Most Recent External Audit Report.
  3. If no external audit is available, applicants should upload:
    • Five months of financial statements covering all organizational accounts.

Preparing these documents in advance can help ensure a smooth application process.

 

Important Information for Applicants

The New Africa Fund has emphasized that:

  • Applications to AFI are completely free.
  • No application fee is charged.
  • Applicants should never pay anyone claiming to guarantee selection into the program.

If anyone requests payment while claiming to represent the New Africa Fund or any of its grant programs, this should be treated as fraud.

Applicants are advised:

  • Do not make any payment.
  • Report fraudulent requests directly to the New Africa Fund.

 

Learn More Before Applying

Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to watch the official AFI informational webinar before completing their application.

The webinar explains:

  • Program structure.
  • Eligibility requirements.
  • Application process.
  • Expectations for selected organizations.
  • Frequently asked questions.

Watching the webinar can help applicants submit stronger and more complete applications.

Click Here for more information.

 

Why Organizations Should Consider Applying

Many nonprofit organizations struggle not because their missions lack impact, but because they lack sustainable fundraising systems.

The Africa Fundraising Incubator goes beyond traditional funding opportunities by helping organizations develop long-term fundraising capacity instead of relying solely on one-time grants.

Participants gain practical experience, professional training, institutional support, and opportunities to build stronger relationships with donors while improving their organizational sustainability.

For organizations seeking to strengthen their fundraising capabilities and expand their impact across Africa, the AFI program offers a valuable opportunity to develop the knowledge, systems, and confidence needed for long-term success.

 

Conclusion

The Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026 provides African organizations with a unique opportunity to strengthen their fundraising capacity through expert training, practical fundraising experience, fiscal sponsorship, leadership development, and access to matching funds.

Rather than focusing only on short-term financial assistance, the program equips organizations with sustainable fundraising skills that can continue generating support long after the program ends. With online training, a live fundraising challenge, up to $5,000 USD in matching funds, 12 months of free fiscal sponsorship, and an opportunity to attend an all-expenses-paid leadership boot camp in Kigali, AFI represents a significant investment in the long-term success of African organizations.

Eligible organizations registered in African Union member states are encouraged to submit their applications before the 14 August 2026 (5:00 PM UTC) deadline and ensure all required documents are ready before beginning the application process.

Ready to Apply? Click Here.

For similar opportunities Click here

The post Africa Fundraising Incubator (AFI) 2026 for African Organizations: Receive Fundraising Training, Up to $5,000 in Matching Funds, 12 Months of Free Fiscal Sponsorship, and an All-Expenses-Paid Leadership Boot Camp in Kigali appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Cardinals Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Climate Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Dora Kumi
item.description:
The climate crisis is one of the defining challenges of our generation, and young people are increasingly leading the way in developing innovative solutions. However, many aspiring climate leaders face barriers such as limited access to mentorship, leadership training, funding, and international networks that can help transform their ideas into lasting impact. The Climate Cardinals […]

The post Climate Cardinals Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Climate Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The climate crisis is one of the defining challenges of our generation, and young people are increasingly leading the way in developing innovative solutions. However, many aspiring climate leaders face barriers such as limited access to mentorship, leadership training, funding, and international networks that can help transform their ideas into lasting impact. The Climate Cardinals Fellowship Program was created to address these challenges by equipping young climate advocates with practical leadership skills, professional development opportunities, project funding, and a global support network.

Each year, the fellowship selects 30 emerging climate leaders from around the world and provides them with a structured learning experience that combines professional training with hands-on leadership. Fellows not only gain theoretical knowledge but also apply what they learn by leading real climate initiatives that make measurable impacts in their communities and beyond.

Whether your passion lies in climate advocacy, environmental education, digital campaigning, project management, or community organizing, this fellowship provides an opportunity to strengthen your leadership while becoming part of one of the world’s largest climate education organizations.

About Climate Cardinals.

Climate Cardinals is among the world’s largest youth-led climate education organizations, with more than 19,000 volunteers spread across 145 countries. The organization believes that addressing climate change requires inclusive leadership and global collaboration. Instead of limiting young people to supporting roles, Climate Cardinals empowers them to design, lead, and implement programs that influence communities worldwide.

Its leadership philosophy follows a “train-the-trainer” model, ensuring that each fellow becomes capable of mentoring future climate leaders, thereby multiplying the organization’s long-term impact. Today, Climate Cardinals continues to build one of the strongest international communities of emerging environmental leaders.

Why the Climate Cardinals Fellowship Exists

Although young people are passionate about solving environmental problems, many struggle with:

  • Limited leadership opportunities
  • Lack of mentorship
  • Financial constraints
  • Insufficient access to professional development
  • Few opportunities to gain practical experience
  • Limited international networking opportunities

The Climate Cardinals Fellowship bridges these gaps by combining education, mentorship, funding, and experiential learning into one comprehensive leadership program.

Rather than simply teaching leadership concepts, the fellowship allows participants to manage real projects from conception to completion while receiving continuous support from experienced mentors and experts.

Climate Cardinals Fellowship Duration

The fellowship runs for one academic year, beginning in October and ending in May.

This extended timeframe allows fellows to gradually build leadership competencies while simultaneously implementing meaningful climate initiatives.

What Fellows Will Learn

One of the strongest aspects of the Climate Cardinals Fellowship is its structured curriculum.

Participants complete specialized leadership modules that prepare them for careers in environmental advocacy, nonprofit management, and social impact.

Topics include:

  • Project Management
  • Leadership Development
  • Fundraising
  • Movement Building
  • Digital Strategy
  • Community Organizing
  • Professional Development
  • Climate Communications
  • Campaign Planning
  • Team Leadership
  • Public Engagement
  • Organizational Development

The curriculum combines:

  • Multimedia learning materials
  • Self-paced (asynchronous) lessons
  • Live learning sessions
  • Expert-led workshops
  • Practical leadership exercises

This blended learning model allows fellows to learn at their own pace while benefiting from live interaction with experienced professionals.

Real Leadership Experience

Unlike many leadership programs that focus only on classroom learning, the Climate Cardinals Fellowship emphasizes practical experience.

Every fellow joins one of the organization’s seven operational teams and takes ownership of a project from beginning to completion.

This means participants gain real-world experience in:

  • Planning initiatives
  • Managing teams
  • Coordinating volunteers
  • Solving organizational challenges
  • Measuring project outcomes
  • Reporting results
  • Collaborating internationally

This practical approach helps participants build confidence while developing skills that employers and graduate schools highly value.

Funding and Financial Support

Climate Cardinals recognizes that financial barriers often prevent talented young leaders from pursuing social impact work.

To increase accessibility, the fellowship provides:

  • Fellow stipends
  • Project microgrants
  • Leadership training
  • Professional development resources
  • Mentorship support
  • Access to international opportunities

This financial support allows fellows to focus on creating meaningful climate impact rather than worrying about project costs.

Global Impact of Previous Fellows

The fellowship has already demonstrated remarkable success.

Previous fellows have contributed to initiatives such as:

Climate Ambassadors Program

A global ambassador initiative involving:

  • More than 1,000 participants
  • Representation from 140 countries

Climate Calling

An international youth climate opportunities platform that has grown to include:

  • More than 1,500 active users
  • Over 80,000 newsletter subscribers

These examples illustrate how fellows move beyond learning and actively contribute to global climate action.

Alumni Success Stories

More than 250 fellowship alumni have gone on to achieve significant milestones.

Former fellows have:

  • Become Rhodes Scholar finalists
  • Founded nonprofit organizations
  • Established successful social enterprises
  • Built ventures with a combined valuation exceeding USD 209 million
  • Secured prestigious awards
  • Received scholarships
  • Earned fellowships
  • Obtained leadership positions within international organizations

These outcomes demonstrate the fellowship’s long-term commitment to developing future global leaders.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Climate Cardinals actively works to make leadership opportunities accessible to underrepresented communities.

According to the program:

  • 77.5% of the 2025–2026 fellows identify as BIPOC.
  • 68% identify as women.

The fellowship also prioritizes young people from:

  • Multilingual communities
  • Immigrant communities
  • Non-English-speaking backgrounds
  • Historically underrepresented populations

This commitment helps ensure that climate leadership reflects the diversity of communities most affected by climate change.

Expected Fellowship Outcomes

The Climate Cardinals Fellowship aims to achieve measurable outcomes each year.

These include:

Leadership Development

Training 30 emerging climate leaders annually through:

  • Experiential learning
  • Mentorship
  • Professional development
  • Practical leadership opportunities

The organization expects at least 80% of fellows to apply their newly acquired skills through leadership roles, expanded projects, or newly launched initiatives within six months after completing the fellowship.

Building Representative Leadership

Climate Cardinals seeks to build an inclusive leadership pipeline by:

  • Investing in long-term mentorship
  • Nominating each fellow for at least one external opportunity such as scholarships, fellowships, awards, conferences, or leadership programs
  • Conducting alumni impact surveys two and five years after graduation

This approach ensures fellows continue receiving support long after the fellowship concludes.

Why You Should Apply

The Climate Cardinals Fellowship offers much more than leadership training.

Participants gain:

  • Practical climate leadership experience
  • International networking opportunities
  • Expert mentorship
  • Professional development support
  • Project funding
  • Leadership workshops
  • Career readiness training
  • Access to a global climate community
  • Opportunities to mentor future fellows
  • Lifelong alumni connections.

Apply HERE.

Learn more about the application HERE.

Deadline: No application deadline was provided.

For young people passionate about climate action, this fellowship provides a unique pathway toward becoming an influential leader capable of creating lasting environmental and social change.

Conclusion

The Climate Cardinals Fellowship Program is an exceptional opportunity for emerging climate leaders who want to transform their passion into measurable impact. Through a combination of structured learning, practical project management, mentorship, financial support, and access to a global network, fellows develop the skills necessary to lead meaningful climate initiatives throughout their careers. By investing in young leaders from diverse backgrounds, Climate Cardinals is helping shape the next generation of environmental changemakers capable of driving sustainable solutions on a global scale. If you are committed to climate action, leadership, and community impact, this fellowship offers the knowledge, experience, and international connections needed to accelerate your journey.

For more Global FELLOWSHIPS click HERE.

The post Climate Cardinals Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Climate Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator 2026: Empowering Young Entrepreneurs to Expand Across African Markets
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator 2026 is a growth-focused initiative designed to help ambitious young entrepreneurs expand established businesses into new African markets. Developed in partnership with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and the African Union (AU), the programme supports export-ready youth-led enterprises seeking to strengthen their export capacity, overcome barriers to intra-African trade […]

The post YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator 2026: Empowering Young Entrepreneurs to Expand Across African Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator 2026 is a growth-focused initiative designed to help ambitious young entrepreneurs expand established businesses into new African markets. Developed in partnership with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and the African Union (AU), the programme supports export-ready youth-led enterprises seeking to strengthen their export capacity, overcome barriers to intra-African trade and scale beyond their domestic markets.

The initiative is particularly aligned with the objectives of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) and its Protocol on Women and Youth in Trade. It seeks to increase the participation of young entrepreneurs in regional commerce while helping businesses become more competitive, investment-ready and capable of creating sustainable employment opportunities.

What Is the YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator?

The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator, also known as the E-Accelerator, is a results-driven programme for young entrepreneurs whose businesses are already established locally and are ready to expand beyond their national borders.

Rather than offering a traditional grant, the programme focuses on strengthening businesses so they can become more prepared for regional trade and international expansion. Selected enterprises may receive tailored support designed around their specific business needs, export potential and growth challenges.

The programme aims to help participating enterprises:

  • Strengthen their export capacity.
  • Prepare for entry into new African markets.
  • Improve their competitiveness.
  • Overcome non-tariff barriers to trade.
  • Navigate complex customs and regulatory requirements.
  • Improve their access to finance and investment opportunities.
  • Build stronger regional business networks.
  • Expand their operations across African markets.
  • Create more productive and sustainable jobs for young people.

Who Is the Programme Designed For?

The 2026 pilot phase is focused on young entrepreneurs and youth-led enterprises operating in six African countries:

  • Rwanda
  • Uganda
  • Senegal
  • The Gambia
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe

Applicants should be under 35 years old and operate an already established business at the local level. The programme is designed for enterprises that are ready to move beyond their domestic markets and explore opportunities for expansion across Africa.

This means the E-Accelerator is particularly suited to entrepreneurs who already have a functioning business and are looking to take the next step toward regional or continental growth.

Important: This Is Not a Grant-Only Opportunity

The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator clearly states that the programme is not designed for applicants who are only looking for grants.

The E-Accelerator is intended for ambitious, growth-oriented businesses that are prepared to invest effort and resources into their own expansion. Applicants should be interested in building a competitive enterprise capable of participating in intra-African trade.

The programme focuses on business development, export readiness, market expansion and access to opportunities rather than simply providing direct financial grants.

How the Export Accelerator Supports African Youth-Led Businesses

Many young enterprises face significant barriers when trying to expand into other countries. These challenges can include complex customs procedures, regulatory requirements, limited knowledge of foreign markets, difficulties accessing finance and weak systems for connecting businesses with potential regional partners.

The E-Accelerator adopts a systemic approach to addressing these challenges.

Instead of offering only general training, the programme is designed to understand the specific needs of participating enterprises and provide support that is tailored to their individual growth and export journeys.

The programme will help qualified businesses work toward:

  • Export readiness.
  • Business strengthening.
  • Market expansion.
  • Improved access to investment and finance.
  • Better understanding of regulatory and trade requirements.
  • Greater ability to navigate non-tariff barriers.
  • Increased participation in intra-African trade.

The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator Journey

The programme consists of several stages designed to take participating enterprises from initial assessment through to export opportunities and continental business exposure.

1. Application and Diagnostics

The process begins with the Youth-Led Enterprise Registration and Diagnostic Form.

Applicants are expected to provide information about their businesses, growth aspirations and current operations. The information collected through the application will help inform the diagnostic process.

The diagnostic stage is important because it provides the foundation for identifying the specific areas where an enterprise may require support.

Applicants are encouraged to provide complete and accurate information, including both mandatory and optional details where applicable. The information may also support future engagement with other relevant opportunities.

2. Selection and Announcement of the Pilot Cohort

Following the application and diagnostic stage, eligible enterprises will be assessed for participation in the pilot cohort.

The selected businesses will be announced as part of the next stage of the programme.

Selection is expected to focus on enterprises with strong growth potential and the capacity or ambition to expand into intra-African markets.

3. Export FitLab

Selected enterprises will participate in the Export FitLab, which is designed to help businesses understand their export potential and identify the steps required to become more competitive in regional markets.

This stage may help enterprises examine their current business models, products, services, market readiness and expansion opportunities.

4. Tailored Enterprise Advisory

Participating businesses will receive individualized support based on their specific needs.

The advisory component is designed to move beyond a one-size-fits-all approach. Instead, support will be tailored to help each enterprise address its own challenges and strengthen its ability to expand.

Possible areas of focus may include:

  • Business growth strategies.
  • Export readiness.
  • Market entry.
  • Regulatory requirements.
  • Trade processes.
  • Access to finance.
  • Business competitiveness.
  • Regional expansion planning.

5. Continental Bootcamp

The programme also includes a Continental Bootcamp during the YouthConnekt Africa Summit.

This stage will provide opportunities for participating entrepreneurs to connect with other young business leaders, gain additional knowledge and engage with broader continental networks.

It may also create opportunities for exposure to potential partners, investors and other stakeholders involved in Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem.

6. Business Export Accreditation

A further stage of the programme is Business Export Accreditation.

This component is intended to support the recognition and strengthening of participating enterprises as businesses that are better prepared for export and regional trade.

Export accreditation can help businesses demonstrate their preparedness and improve their credibility when engaging with potential markets and partners.

7. Facilitated Intra-African Export Through Policy Sandboxes

One of the distinctive features of the E-Accelerator is the use of Policy Sandboxes.

These sandboxes are intended to help qualified enterprises navigate and address barriers that can make cross-border trade difficult.

The approach will begin with the six pilot countries:

  • Rwanda
  • Uganda
  • Senegal
  • The Gambia
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe

By addressing practical trade challenges, the programme aims to facilitate smoother export processes and improve the ability of youth-led businesses to participate in intra-African markets.

8. Exposure to Continental Business and Financing Opportunities

The final component of the journey connects participating enterprises with wider business and financing opportunities across the continent.

This may help businesses build relationships, explore new markets, identify potential investment opportunities and connect with relevant stakeholders who can support their expansion.

Why This Programme Matters for Young African Entrepreneurs

Young entrepreneurs are central to Africa’s economic transformation, but many youth-led businesses remain focused on local markets because of the challenges involved in expanding across borders.

The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator aims to help address some of these challenges by supporting businesses that have already established a local foundation and are ready to grow.

The programme can help participating entrepreneurs move from simply operating a business in one market to exploring opportunities in the wider African economy.

Its broader objectives include:

  • Supporting youth participation in regional trade.
  • Strengthening youth-led enterprises.
  • Supporting the implementation of the AfCFTA.
  • Addressing non-tariff barriers.
  • Improving business competitiveness.
  • Mobilizing investment.
  • Supporting sustainable job creation.
  • Expanding opportunities for intra-African commerce.

What Applicants Should Know Before Applying

The application form is described as lengthy because it is designed to collect detailed information about the applicant and the enterprise.

Applicants should therefore be prepared to provide information that can help programme organizers understand:

  • The nature of their business.
  • The current stage of business development.
  • Their growth ambitions.
  • Their readiness to expand beyond their domestic market.
  • Their potential export opportunities.
  • The challenges affecting their expansion.
  • Their interest in the programme’s value proposition.

Applicants should complete the form carefully and provide accurate information. The quality and completeness of the information submitted may help inform the diagnostic process and future tailored advisory support.

No Participation Fees

YouthConnekt Africa states that it does not charge fees for participation in its programmes.

Applicants should also be aware that neither YouthConnekt Africa staff nor its partners should request payment or contact applicants through personal channels regarding programme applications.

Applicants are advised to verify the authenticity of any communication they receive and rely only on official communication channels.

How to Apply

Interested young entrepreneurs should begin by reviewing the programme information and completing the official YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator registration and diagnostic form.

Applicants should:

  1. Confirm that they meet the basic age and country requirements.
  2. Ensure that their enterprise is already established locally.
  3. Consider whether their business is genuinely ready for growth beyond national borders.
  4. Review the programme’s value proposition carefully.
  5. Complete the application and diagnostic questions accurately.
  6. Provide as much relevant information as possible to support the diagnostic process.
  7. Submit the form through the official application process.

The programme is intended for entrepreneurs who are serious about expanding their businesses and participating in the wider African market.

𝗗𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲

𝟭𝟲 𝗔𝘂𝗴𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟲

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator 2026 offers an opportunity for ambitious young entrepreneurs in Rwanda, Uganda, Senegal, The Gambia, Zambia and Zimbabwe to strengthen their businesses and prepare for expansion into new African markets.

Through diagnostics, tailored enterprise advisory, export readiness support, accreditation, policy sandboxes, continental networking and exposure to business and financing opportunities, the programme seeks to help promising youth-led enterprises overcome some of the barriers that limit their participation in intra-African trade.

For entrepreneurs who already have an established business and are ready to explore regional expansion, the E-Accelerator could provide valuable support in turning local business potential into broader continental opportunities.

For more information, visit the official call for applications

Discover more global accelerator opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post YouthConnekt Africa Export Accelerator 2026: Empowering Young Entrepreneurs to Expand Across African Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowships September 2026: Fully Funded International Research Mobility Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
The ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowship Programme 2026 is now accepting applications from eligible researchers in ICGEB Member States. Offered by the International Centre for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (ICGEB), this prestigious fellowship promotes international scientific mobility by supporting researchers to undertake advanced research training in another ICGEB Member State. The fellowship is designed to […]

The post ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowships September 2026: Fully Funded International Research Mobility Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowship Programme 2026 is now accepting applications from eligible researchers in ICGEB Member States. Offered by the International Centre for Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology (ICGEB), this prestigious fellowship promotes international scientific mobility by supporting researchers to undertake advanced research training in another ICGEB Member State.

The fellowship is designed to strengthen scientific cooperation, develop technical expertise, and encourage long-term collaboration between research institutions. Successful applicants will spend between 3 and 9 months at a host laboratory, gaining hands-on experience with cutting-edge technologies while contributing to collaborative research projects in the life sciences.

Whether you are a PhD student, postdoctoral researcher, or an early-career scientist, this fellowship offers a valuable opportunity to expand your research skills, build international collaborations, and advance your scientific career.

About the ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowship Programme

The Scientific Mobility for Advanced Research Training (SMART) Fellowship Programme was established to promote research collaboration between laboratories located in different ICGEB Member States. The programme enables scientists to receive practical training in technologies and methodologies that may not be available in their home institutions.

A major objective of the programme is to strengthen scientific partnerships. Therefore, applicants are expected to demonstrate a strong collaborative research project jointly prepared by both the home and receiving laboratories. Supervisors from both institutions are strongly encouraged to work together during the proposal development stage to ensure a high-quality application.

Applications are welcome in all areas of the Life Sciences, making this fellowship suitable for researchers from diverse scientific disciplines.

 Fellowship Duration

Successful applicants will receive funding for research visits lasting:

Minimum duration: 3 months
Maximum duration: 9 months

The fellowship supports temporary scientific mobility and is not intended for long-term degree programmes or extensions of existing fellowships.

Fellowship Benefits

The ICGEB SMART Fellowship provides comprehensive financial support to selected fellows, including:

  •  International economy-class return airfare
  •  Monthly living stipend
  • Health insurance
  •  Research mobility support
  •  Access to internationally recognized laboratories
  •  Hands-on training using advanced technologies
  •  Opportunities to establish long-term international scientific collaborations

 Monthly Stipend

Selected fellows receive:

USD 1,500 per month for PhD fellows
USD 2,000 per month for Postdoctoral fellows

In addition, a USD 500 monthly bench fee may be provided directly to the receiving laboratory to support research consumables required for the approved research project.

Eligible Research Areas

Applications are accepted in any area of the Life Sciences, including but not limited to:

  •  Biotechnology
  • Molecular Biology
  • Genetics
  •  Biomedical Sciences
  •  Agricultural Biotechnology
  •  Environmental Sciences
  •  Cell Biology
  •  Microbiology
  •  Biochemistry
  •  Bioinformatics

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  •  Be nationals of an ICGEB Member State.
  • Be actively working in a recognized research institution.
  •  Be registered for a PhD or have obtained a PhD within the last five years.
  •  Young scientists holding an MSc or an equivalent qualification are also encouraged to apply.
  • Demonstrate a clear research collaboration between their home and receiving laboratories
  •  Apply to undertake research in another ICGEB Member State (not their own).

There is no age limit for applicants. However, preference is given to scientists at the early stages of their research careers.

SMART Fellowships are not intended to:

  •  Support attendance at conferences or workshops.
  •  Extend previous fellowship visits.
  •  Provide bridge funding before or after another fellowship programme.

New Two-Stage Application Process

ICGEB has introduced a new application procedure consisting of two stages.

Stage 1: Fellowship Expression of Interest

Application Period: 1 July – 14 August 2026

Applicants must:

  • Select their preferred host laboratory.
  •  Identify a prospective supervisor.
  •  Submit an online Expression of Interest through the ICGEB Service Gateway.

Only applicants who complete this stage successfully will proceed to the next phase.

Stage 2: Full Fellowship Application

Application Period: 16 – 30 September 2026

Applicants selected by their prospective supervisors will receive an invitation to submit the complete fellowship application through the ICGEB Service Gateway.

Required Documents

Applicants will need to upload the following documents:

  •  Full Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  •  CV of the Head of the Receiving Laboratory (including publications)
  •  Valid passport or national identity card

Applicants should also ensure that both the Home Laboratory and Receiving Laboratory complete their respective sections before the application deadline. Applications lacking confirmation from both laboratories will not be considered.

Selection Criteria

Applications are reviewed by the ICGEB Fellowships Selection Committee based on:

  •  Scientific excellence of the proposed project
  • Applicant’s academic and research achievements
  •  Expected career impact
  •  Benefits to both home and host institution
  • Suitability of the host laboratory
  • Availability of expertise and research facilities
  •  Strength of the collaboration between institutions

Additional institutional support from the host laboratory, such as accommodation or supplementary living support, may positively influence the evaluation.

Bench Fee Support

A monthly **USD 500 bench fee** may be awarded to the host laboratory for laboratory consumables directly related to the approved research project.

Eligible expenses include:

  • Laboratory reagents
  •  Chemicals
  •  Cell culture materials
  •  PCR and qPCR consumables
  •  Laboratory plastics and glassware
  •  Pipettes and laboratory consumables

The funding cannot be used for equipment purchases, travel expenses, administrative costs, office supplies, software, or institutional overheads.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to allow both laboratories sufficient

How to Apply

Interested applicant time to complete the required confirmations.

Applicants should carefully read the official application guidelines before beginning the online application process through the ICGEB Service Gateway. For detailed guidelines, application forms, FAQs, and additional fellowship resources, visit the official ICGEB fellowship 

Conclusion

The ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowship Programme 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for early-career researchers to gain international research experience while strengthening scientific collaboration across ICGEB Member States. Through generous financial support, advanced laboratory training, and international networking opportunities, the fellowship enables researchers to enhance their expertise and contribute meaningfully to scientific innovation in the life sciences. Eligible scientists are encouraged to prepare their applications early and take advantage of this prestigious international research opportunity.

Discover more fellowship opportunities like this on the OFY Webpage.

The post ICGEB Arturo Falaschi SMART Fellowships September 2026: Fully Funded International Research Mobility Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027: Receive $12,000 in Funding to Advance Research on Russia and Eurasia (Fully Virtual)
item.dc:creator: Priyanka Kharbanda
item.description:
Researchers, academics, journalists, and policy experts with a strong interest in Russia and the broader Eurasian region now have an exceptional opportunity to advance their work through the Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027. This prestigious international fellowship is designed to promote original scholarship, encourage policy-relevant research, and strengthen intellectual exchange across borders through an entirely […]

The post The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027: Receive $12,000 in Funding to Advance Research on Russia and Eurasia (Fully Virtual) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers, academics, journalists, and policy experts with a strong interest in Russia and the broader Eurasian region now have an exceptional opportunity to advance their work through the Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027. This prestigious international fellowship is designed to promote original scholarship, encourage policy-relevant research, and strengthen intellectual exchange across borders through an entirely virtual program.

Supported generously by the Lounsbery Foundation and the U.S. Russia Foundation, the fellowship provides six months of financial support, professional mentorship, networking opportunities, and publication assistance for outstanding scholars and experts from around the world.

Unlike traditional residential fellowships, the Digital Fellowship is conducted entirely online, making it accessible to participants regardless of their geographic location. Fellows become part of an international community of researchers while contributing meaningful work that reaches policymakers, scholars, and the broader public.

If your work focuses on Russia, Eurasia, regional politics, technology, medicine, science, history, international relations, journalism, or related fields, this fellowship offers both financial support and an influential platform to amplify your research.

About the Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship

The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship was created to strengthen international scholarly collaboration and promote high-quality research focused on Russia and the former Soviet Union.

The fellowship responds to the growing need for renewed intellectual engagement between scholars from the former Soviet region and the wider international community. Through its fully digital format, the program removes geographical barriers and enables researchers worldwide to collaborate, exchange ideas, and contribute to informed policy discussions.

Each fellowship lasts six months and combines financial assistance with professional development, mentorship, editorial support, and networking opportunities.

Participants will work closely with the Kennan Institute throughout the fellowship while developing research projects designed to inform both academic audiences and policymakers.

Fellowship Objectives

The Digital Fellowship seeks to achieve several important objectives, including:

  • Supporting original, high-quality scholarship on Russia and Eurasia.
  • Encouraging policy-relevant research that contributes to informed public discussions.
  • Providing researchers with time and financial resources to complete meaningful projects.
  • Strengthening communication between scholars and policymakers.
  • Helping fellows publish accessible research for broader audiences.
  • Creating lasting international research collaborations.
  • Expanding the global network of experts studying Russia and Eurasia.
  • Building a vibrant alumni community for continued collaboration after the fellowship concludes.

Number of Fellowships Available

The Kennan Institute will award:

  • 10 Digital Fellowships during the 2026–2027 fellowship cycle.

Importantly,

  • At least four fellowships are reserved for applicants conducting research related to:
    • Natural Sciences
    • Medicine
    • Technology
    • Related scientific disciplines

This ensures interdisciplinary representation alongside traditional humanities and social science research.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive an extensive package of financial and professional support.

1. Monthly Research Stipend

Every selected fellow will receive:

  • $2,000 per month

Since the fellowship runs for six months, successful applicants may receive a total of approximately:

  • $12,000 in financial support

This funding is intended to allow participants to dedicate significant time to their research activities.

2. Professional Mentorship

Participants will benefit from guidance provided by the Kennan Institute’s network of internationally recognized experts.

Mentorship may include:

  • Research advice
  • Policy engagement
  • Publication guidance
  • Academic networking
  • Professional development

3. Monthly Virtual Meetings

Fellows will participate in:

  • Monthly virtual cohort meetings
  • Discussions with fellow researchers
  • Collaborative knowledge sharing
  • Research progress updates
  • Peer feedback sessions

These meetings help create a collaborative international research community.

4. Specialized Webinars

Participants will receive access to:

  • Six virtual webinars

The webinars are tailored specifically to the interests and needs of each fellowship cohort.

Topics may include:

  • Research communication
  • Publishing strategies
  • Policy engagement
  • Public scholarship
  • Career development
  • Academic networking

5. Publication Support

An important feature of the fellowship is assistance with communicating research to broader audiences.

Each fellow will work with Kennan Institute staff to develop and edit a final publication intended for:

  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Journalists
  • The general public

Possible publication formats include:

  • Research articles
  • Multimedia projects
  • Documentary or short films
  • Digital exhibitions
  • Other public-facing formats

6. Global Alumni Network

Upon completion of the fellowship, participants become members of the Kennan Institute’s distinguished alumni network, which includes:

  • More than 4,500 professionals worldwide

This network offers long-term opportunities for collaboration, research partnerships, and professional growth.

Fellowship Timeline

The 2026–2027 Digital Fellowship will run from:

October 1, 2026 – March 31, 2027

Following the fellowship period, participants may also have opportunities to engage in additional Kennan Institute events.

Research Areas Supported

The fellowship welcomes proposals covering Russia and the broader Eurasian region across numerous disciplines.

Examples include:

  • Political science
  • International relations
  • History
  • Economics
  • Sociology
  • Security studies
  • Public policy
  • Journalism
  • Technology
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Medicine
  • Environmental science
  • Natural sciences
  • Regional studies
  • Cultural studies
  • Media studies

Interdisciplinary research proposals are also encouraged.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is intended for experienced professionals whose work centers on Russia or the broader Eurasian region.

Ideal applicants include:

  • University researchers
  • Faculty members
  • Independent scholars
  • Journalists
  • Policy analysts
  • Think tank researchers
  • Subject matter experts
  • Scientific researchers
  • Technology specialists
  • Medical researchers

Applicants should demonstrate meaningful experience within their chosen research area.

Apply here

Fellowship Requirements

During the fellowship, selected participants are expected to:

  • Attend monthly virtual meetings.
  • Participate actively in cohort discussions.
  • Collaborate with Kennan Institute staff.
  • Develop their research project throughout the fellowship.
  • Produce a publication or public-facing research output.
  • Share findings with wider academic and policy communities.

Language Requirement

The fellowship is conducted entirely in:

  • English

Applicants should therefore possess a good working knowledge of English.

Research outputs prepared during the fellowship will initially be published in English, although future translations into regional languages may be possible.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must complete the online application and submit:

  • Completed application form
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV) or Resume
  • Research Proposal (maximum three pages)

The research proposal should include:

  • Previous experience with the proposed research topic.
  • Scope of the project.
  • Research methodology.
  • Expected outcomes.
  • Preferred format for presenting findings (such as an article, multimedia project, exhibit, or film).

Applicants will also indicate whether their proposed project continues previous work or represents an entirely new line of inquiry.

What the Fellowship Does Not Cover

Because the fellowship is conducted entirely online:

The Kennan Institute does not provide:

  • Travel expenses
  • Visa costs
  • Other travel-related funding

No physical attendance is required during the fellowship.

Why You Should Apply

The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship offers far more than financial support.

Participants gain:

  • Significant research funding
  • International recognition
  • Editorial and publication assistance
  • Direct engagement with policy experts
  • Professional mentorship
  • Access to an influential global alumni network
  • Opportunities to share research with policymakers
  • Long-term academic and professional connections

For researchers seeking to expand both the quality and visibility of their work on Russia and Eurasia, this fellowship represents a valuable opportunity.

Application Deadline

Applicants must submit all required materials no later than August 14, 2026.

Late applications are unlikely to be considered, so interested candidates should prepare their documents well in advance.

Visit here to learn more about the The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027

Conclusion

The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027 provides an outstanding opportunity for researchers, journalists, academics, and policy professionals dedicated to advancing knowledge about Russia and the broader Eurasian region. Through generous financial support, expert mentorship, publication opportunities, and access to an extensive international network, the fellowship enables participants to produce impactful research while engaging with leading scholars and policymakers worldwide. As a fully virtual program, it is accessible to qualified applicants across the globe, making it one of the most inclusive and valuable international research fellowships available. Interested applicants should ensure that all required documents are submitted before August 14, 2026, to be considered for this prestigious opportunity.

For more details, please visit OFY here

The post The Kennan Institute Digital Fellowship 2026–2027: Receive $12,000 in Funding to Advance Research on Russia and Eurasia (Fully Virtual) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026: Win Up to USD 750 by Showcasing the Power of TVET Through Photography
item.dc:creator: Fridah Kamuri
item.description:
The UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026 is now open for submissions, inviting both amateur and professional photographers from around the world to showcase how Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) is transforming lives, empowering young people, and creating sustainable futures. The international competition offers photographers the opportunity to have their work recognized […]

The post UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026: Win Up to USD 750 by Showcasing the Power of TVET Through Photography appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026 is now open for submissions, inviting both amateur and professional photographers from around the world to showcase how Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) is transforming lives, empowering young people, and creating sustainable futures. The international competition offers photographers the opportunity to have their work recognized by UNESCO while competing for cash prizes of up to USD 750.

Photography has long served as a powerful storytelling tool capable of documenting change, inspiring action, and highlighting the realities of communities across the world. Through this year’s competition, UNESCO-UNEVOC seeks compelling visual stories that demonstrate how vocational education equips individuals with practical skills, promotes innovation, strengthens inclusion, advances sustainability, and prepares young people for the future of work.

Whether you capture a young engineer, a green energy technician, a robotics student, or a skilled artisan, your photograph could become part of UNESCO’s global efforts to promote the importance of skills development.

Applications are now open, with submissions accepted until 31 August 2026.

About the UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition

The Skills in Action Photo Competition is organized by UNESCO-UNEVOC, UNESCO’s International Centre for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). The initiative celebrates the transformative impact of vocational education by encouraging photographers to capture authentic moments that reflect how technical and vocational skills contribute to:

  • Innovation
  • Social inclusion
  • Sustainable development
  • Green economies
  • Digital transformation
  • Youth employment
  • Equal opportunities

The competition aims to increase global awareness of TVET while providing photographers with an international platform to showcase their creativity and storytelling abilities.

Successful entries will highlight how practical skills are helping individuals and communities address current and future global challenges.

Competition Categories

Participants may submit photographs under one or more of the following four competition themes.

1. Equity and Inclusion

Photographs in this category should demonstrate that skills development opportunities should be accessible to everyone regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Nationality
  • Ethnicity
  • Disability
  • Socio-economic background

Participants are encouraged to capture stories that reflect diversity, equal opportunities, and inclusive vocational education.

2. Green Skills and Sustainability

This category highlights the growing importance of environmentally sustainable skills.

Entries may feature:

  • Renewable energy
  • Green jobs
  • Sustainable agriculture
  • Eco-friendly technologies
  • Circular economy initiatives
  • Resource-efficient practices
  • Climate-smart vocational training

The objective is to illustrate how TVET contributes to environmental sustainability and climate resilience.

3. Digital Transformation and Artificial Intelligence

The future of work increasingly depends on digital skills.

UNESCO welcomes photographs showing young people participating in:

  • Coding
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Robotics
  • Automation
  • Virtual reality
  • Digital manufacturing
  • Emerging technologies

Images should reflect how digital transformation is shaping vocational education.

4. Innovation and Excellence

This category recognizes creative approaches to vocational education and skills development.

Suitable entries may showcase:

  • Innovative teaching methods
  • Modern vocational training facilities
  • Technical excellence
  • Productive workplaces
  • Creative problem-solving
  • Advanced industrial practices

The photographs should demonstrate how innovation improves quality, productivity, and efficiency.

How Many Entries Can You Submit?

Participants may submit:

  • One photograph per competition category.
  • A maximum of four photographs in total.

Each submission must correspond to one of the official competition themes.

Competition Prizes

Outstanding photographers will receive the following cash awards:

  • First Prize: USD 750
  • Second Prize: USD 500
  • Third Prize: USD 350

In addition to the monetary awards, winners will also receive:

  • An official UNESCO-UNEVOC certificate.
  • Publication of their winning photographs on the UNESCO-UNEVOC Flickr account.
  • Promotion across UNESCO-UNEVOC’s official social media platforms.
  • International exposure through UNESCO’s communication channels.

Winning the competition offers photographers an opportunity to gain worldwide recognition while contributing to UNESCO’s advocacy for technical and vocational education.

Ownership of Submitted Photographs

Applicants should note that all submitted photographs become the property of UNESCO-UNEVOC.

The organization may use submitted images in:

  • Future publications
  • Promotional materials
  • Media campaigns
  • Awareness campaigns
  • Educational resources
  • Communication activities

Participants should therefore ensure they fully understand these terms before submitting their work.

Eligibility Requirements

To participate, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be at least 18 years old.
  • Be the owner or copyright holder of every submitted photograph.

Failure to satisfy these conditions may result in disqualification.

Photo Submission Requirements

Every photo submission must include:

  • A photo title.
  • A brief description of no more than 75 words.
  • Information indicating where the photograph was taken.
  • Information indicating when the photograph was taken.
  • A completed submission form.

Providing complete and accurate information helps judges understand the context behind each image.

Technical File Specifications

Submitted photographs must satisfy the following technical requirements:

  • JPEG format is mandatory.
  • RAW or TIFF files may be included as supplementary files and are considered an advantage.
  • The longest side of the image must measure at least 3,000 pixels.
  • Minor digital adjustments are permitted, provided they are disclosed in the submission form.

Applicants are encouraged to submit high-quality images that preserve important details and visual clarity.

Competition Do’s

Participants are encouraged to:

  • Select one or more official competition categories.
  • Submit high-resolution images.
  • Ensure the longest side measures at least 3,000 pixels.
  • Maintain a resolution of 300 DPI/PPI.
  • Obtain consent from all identifiable individuals appearing in the photographs.
  • Declare all digital edits in the submission form.
  • Submit photographs in JPEG format.
  • Include RAW or TIFF files where available.

Following these recommendations will improve compliance with competition requirements.

Competition Don’ts

Participants should avoid the following:

  • Submitting photo collages.
  • Combining multiple images into one frame.
  • Adding logos.
  • Adding watermarks.
  • Adding signatures.
  • Adding graphic overlays.
  • Submitting photographs unrelated to the official themes.
  • Using AI-generated images.
  • Using artificial intelligence to generate, extend, or manipulate parts of a photograph.

Failure to comply with these rules may result in disqualification.

How to Submit Your Entry

Applicants should carefully follow the submission process below.

Step 1

Complete and sign the official submission form.

Be sure to indicate the competition category for each photograph.

Step 2

Prepare your email submission.

The subject line should include:

Your Last Name – SAPC26 Submission

Example:

Muller – SAPC26 Submission

Step 3

Attach:

  • Your completed submission form.
  • Your photograph(s).

Step 4

Send your submission by email to:

unevoc.skills@unesco.org

Step 5

If your files exceed email size limits:

  • Upload them to a cloud storage platform.
  • Include a publicly accessible sharing link.
  • Ensure the link does not require login credentials, permission requests, or additional approvals.

Selection Criteria

All eligible entries will be evaluated based on the following criteria:

  • Relevance to the competition theme.
  • Originality.
  • Creativity.
  • Innovative communication of the intended message.

The judging panel will assess both the artistic quality of the photograph and its ability to communicate the importance of technical and vocational education.

Announcement of Winners

The winners will be officially announced through UNESCO-UNEVOC’s digital platforms.

Successful participants should monitor UNESCO-UNEVOC’s official communication channels for announcements and updates following the close of the competition.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition provides far more than an opportunity to win cash prizes. It offers photographers the chance to:

  • Gain international recognition.
  • Showcase their storytelling abilities.
  • Promote the importance of vocational education.
  • Inspire future generations.
  • Contribute to UNESCO’s global education initiatives.
  • Expand their professional portfolio.
  • Reach worldwide audiences through UNESCO’s international platforms.

Whether you are documenting innovation in manufacturing, women breaking barriers in engineering, sustainable agriculture, digital technology, or youth entrepreneurship, your images can help shape global conversations on the future of skills.

Visit HERE for more information about the UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026

Application Deadline

Submission Deadline: 31 August 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their entries well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical challenges.

APPLY HERE

Conclusion

The UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for photographers to combine creativity with global impact. By capturing authentic stories of Technical and Vocational Education and Training, participants can demonstrate how skills development is driving innovation, promoting inclusion, supporting sustainability, and preparing young people for the rapidly evolving world of work.

With attractive cash prizes, international recognition, and the opportunity to have your work featured by UNESCO, this competition is ideal for photographers passionate about education, youth empowerment, sustainable development, and visual storytelling. If you have compelling images that reflect the transformative power of TVET, now is the time to share them with a global audience before the 31 August 2026 deadline.

For more global Competition opportunities visit OFY HERE

The post UNESCO-UNEVOC Skills in Action Photo Competition 2026: Win Up to USD 750 by Showcasing the Power of TVET Through Photography appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027 Applications Open | Scholarships & Financial Aid Available
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027, offering outstanding young Africans the opportunity to join one of the continent’s most prestigious leadership and academic institutions. Successful applicants will study in ALA’s renowned two-year diploma program while accessing generous financial aid, with 97% of students receiving scholarships or other financial […]

The post African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027 Applications Open | Scholarships & Financial Aid Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
