item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027, offering outstanding young Africans the opportunity to join one of the continent’s most prestigious leadership and academic institutions. Successful applicants will study in ALA’s renowned two-year diploma program while accessing generous financial aid, with 97% of students receiving scholarships or other financial assistance.

Applicants must have been born on or after 1 September 2007 to be eligible for admission. The program combines world-class academics with leadership development, preparing students to tackle Africa’s most pressing social, economic, and political challenges. Annual tuition is valued at US$30,900, covering tuition, accommodation, meals, and uniforms, with financial support awarded according to each family’s demonstrated financial need.

Key Details

  • Institution: African Leadership Academy (ALA)
  • Program: Two-Year Diploma Program
  • Application Opens: 15 July 2026
  • Early Decision Deadline: 15 October 2026
  • Regular Application Deadline: 15 January 2027
  • Eligible Applicants: Students born on or after 1 September 2007
  • Financial Aid: Available to all admitted students based on financial need
  • Scholarship Coverage: Approximately 97% of students receive financial assistance
  • Annual Tuition Value: US$30,900 (includes tuition, accommodation, meals, and uniforms)
  • Location: Johannesburg, South Africa
  • Target Applicants: Outstanding young African leaders with academic excellence, leadership potential, and a passion for creating positive change.

About African Leadership Academy

African Leadership Academy is dedicated to identifying and developing Africa’s most promising young leaders. Rather than focusing solely on academic excellence, ALA seeks students who demonstrate initiative, resilience, leadership potential, and a genuine desire to create positive change within their communities and across the continent.

Students become part of a diverse pan-African community where they collaborate with peers from different cultures, backgrounds, and experiences while preparing for admission to leading universities worldwide.

Who Should Apply?

ALA is looking for young Africans who are:

  • High-performing students with strong academic potential
  • Passionate about solving problems in their communities
  • Courageous, resilient, and willing to embrace challenges
  • Curious, innovative, and eager to learn
  • Committed to making a lasting impact across Africa
  • Excited to collaborate with talented peers from across the continent

For admission into the Class of 2027, applicants must have been born on or after 1 September 2007.

African Leadership Academy Selection Criteria

ALA evaluates applicants using a holistic admissions process built around four key leadership qualities.

1. Intellectual Readiness

Successful applicants demonstrate:

  • Strong academic performance
  • Critical thinking and problem-solving skills
  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Passion for lifelong learning
  • Ability to thrive in a challenging academic environment

2. Courage and Perseverance

Applicants should show:

  • Initiative to pursue ambitious goals
  • Resilience when facing setbacks
  • Determination to overcome obstacles
  • Self-motivation and personal responsibility

3. Ownership

ALA seeks students who:

  • Dream big and set ambitious goals
  • Demonstrate leadership in their communities
  • Want to create meaningful social impact
  • Are committed to Africa’s future development

4. Interdependence

Strong candidates also:

  • Value teamwork and collaboration
  • Appreciate diverse perspectives
  • Build meaningful relationships
  • Seek support when needed
  • Practice humility and gratitude

African Leadership Academy Financial Aid

Making quality education accessible remains central to ALA’s mission.

Key financial aid facts include:

  • 97% of students receive financial assistance
  • Scholarships are available regardless of nationality
  • Awards are determined after admission decisions
  • Financial support is based on demonstrated family need
  • Tuition covers accommodation, meals, uniforms, and academic fees

Families contribute according to their financial ability, ensuring deserving students can attend regardless of economic background.

African Leadership Academy 2027 Application Timeline

Applicants should take note of the following important dates:

  • Applications Open: 15 July 2026
  • Early Decision Deadline: 15 October 2026
  • Regular Decision Deadline: 15 January 2027
  • Early Decision Results: January 2027
  • Regular Decision Results: April 2027

Submitting an Early Decision application allows applicants to receive their admissions decision sooner.

Nominate an Outstanding Young Leader

ALA also welcomes nominations from teachers, parents, mentors, and community members.

NORMINATE SOMEONE HERE

If you know a young African with exceptional leadership potential, you can nominate them to encourage their application. The academy reviews nominations and contacts selected students, inviting them to apply for admission.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

African Leadership Academy maintains a non-discrimination admissions policy and welcomes qualified applicants from all backgrounds. Admission decisions are based solely on applicants’ abilities, leadership potential, and overall suitability for the program, regardless of gender, race, religion, disability, nationality, ethnicity, wealth, or income.

Why Apply to African Leadership Academy?

Studying at African Leadership Academy provides students with:

  • A world-class leadership-focused education
  • Access to generous scholarships and financial aid
  • A diverse pan-African learning community
  • Intensive leadership development
  • Preparation for leading universities worldwide
  • Lifelong membership in the ALA global network
  • Opportunities to address Africa’s biggest challenges through innovation and service

Young Africans with bold ideas, strong leadership potential, and a passion for creating positive change are encouraged to apply before the application deadlines.

Application Deadline: 15 January 2027 (Regular Decision)

Financial Aid: Available to all admitted students based on demonstrated financial need.

APPLY NOW 

The post African Leadership Academy (ALA) Class of 2027 Applications Open | Scholarships & Financial Aid Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027: 197 Fully Funded Undergraduate, Master’s & PhD Scholarships Across East Africa
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA) has opened applications for the East African Community (EAC) Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) 2026/2027, offering 197 scholarships for Diploma, Undergraduate, Master’s and PhD studies at 28 universities across East Africa. The scholarship enables students from eligible East African Community countries to study in another EAC Partner State […]

The post EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027: 197 Fully Funded Undergraduate, Master’s & PhD Scholarships Across East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA) has opened applications for the East African Community (EAC) Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) 2026/2027, offering 197 scholarships for Diploma, Undergraduate, Master’s and PhD studies at 28 universities across East Africa.

The scholarship enables students from eligible East African Community countries to study in another EAC Partner State while receiving financial support for tuition, travel, health insurance and research costs. Applications close on 20 August 2026.

Key Details

Scholarship EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) 2026/2027
Host Organisation Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA)
Study Levels Diploma, Undergraduate, Master’s, PhD
Number of Scholarships 197
Participating Universities 28 Universities
Host Countries Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania & Uganda
Eligible Applicants Nationals and residents of EAC Partner States
Scholarship Benefits Tuition waiver, examination fees, travel, health insurance, research support (Master’s & PhD)
Application Fee None
Deadline 20 August 2026

What is the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS)?

The East African Community Student Mobility Scholarship Scheme (EAC-SMS) is a regional initiative coordinated by the Inter-University Council for East Africa (IUCEA). The programme supports student mobility by providing scholarships for learners to study in an EAC Partner State other than their own, strengthening collaboration among universities and enhancing regional integration through higher education.

For the 2026/2027 academic year, the programme offers scholarships at 28 universities across Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, and Uganda.

EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Benefits

The scholarship operates as a cost-sharing programme, ensuring students receive significant financial support throughout their studies.

Host Universities Cover

  • 100% Tuition fees
  • Examination fees

IUCEA Covers

  • International travel expenses
  • Health insurance
  • Research support for Master’s and PhD students

Students/Parents Cover

  • Accommodation
  • Daily living expenses
  • Other university-related costs not covered by the scholarship

Number of Scholarships Available

A total of 197 scholarships are available for the 2026/2027 intake, distributed as follows:

Study Level Scholarships
Undergraduate 95
Diploma 1
Master’s 90
PhD 11
Total 197

These opportunities span 164 academic programmes offered by 28 universities across six East African countries.

Participating Countries

Scholarships are available at universities in:

  • Burundi
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda

Students must study in a country different from their home country within the East African Community.

Available Degree Programmes

Applicants can choose from a diverse range of academic disciplines, including:

  • Computer Science
  • Information Technology
  • Cyber Security
  • Statistics
  • Medicine
  • Nursing
  • Public Health
  • Renewable Energy
  • Agriculture
  • Environmental Science
  • Business Administration
  • Economics
  • Finance
  • Engineering
  • Biotechnology
  • Climate Change
  • Education
  • Social Work
  • International Studies
  • Peace and Conflict Studies
  • Tourism
  • Development Studies
  • Law
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Regional Integration
  • Project Planning and Management
  • Monitoring and Evaluation
  • Health Informatics
  • Food Security
  • Sustainable Agriculture
  • Astronomy
  • Swahili Studies

In total, applicants can select from 164 programmes hosted by universities across the East African Community.

Who Can Apply?

To qualify for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027, applicants must:

  • Be a national and resident of an EAC Partner State.
  • Apply to study in another EAC Partner State (not their own).
  • Meet the admission requirements of the chosen university.
  • Demonstrate excellent academic performance.
  • Hold at least an Upper Second-Class Honours degree (or equivalent) for Master’s and PhD programmes.
  • Be below 35 years of age for Master’s programmes.
  • Be below 45 years of age for PhD programmes.
  • Relevant work experience is an added advantage for PhD applicants.

APPLY NOW

Eligible countries include:

  • Burundi
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda

Required Application Documents

Applicants should prepare the following documents:

  • Motivation letter
  • Updated and signed Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Certified academic certificates and transcripts
  • University application form and supporting attachments
  • Passport-size photograph
  • Proof of nationality
  • Proof of residency
  • Evidence of leadership, awards, or community service (where applicable)
  • Letters of reference or other supporting documents (if available)

Foreign qualifications must be officially equated by the relevant national higher education authority, while documents not in English must be accompanied by certified English translations.

How to Apply for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship

The application process consists of two key stages:

Step 1: Select Your Programme

Browse the official list of eligible universities and programmes and choose your preferred course of study. APPLY NOW

Step 2: Submit Your Scholarship Application

Complete the online IUCEA scholarship application and upload all required documents. APPLY NOW

Important: Applicants should not apply directly to universities or pay university application fees. IUCEA coordinates university admissions for successful scholarship applicants.

Important Information

  • No university application fee is required.
  • Applicants may apply to more than one participating university.
  • Only one scholarship will ultimately be awarded to each successful applicant.
  • Students already admitted to a participating university remain eligible to apply for the scholarship.

EAC Student Mobility Scholarship Deadline

Application Deadline: 20 August 2026

Late applications will not be considered. Applicants are encouraged to submit all required documents well before the deadline.

Why Apply for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship?

The EAC Student Mobility Scholarship offers much more than financial support. Scholars benefit from:

  • Studying at leading universities across East Africa.
  • Building regional and international professional networks.
  • Gaining cross-cultural academic experiences.
  • Enhancing career prospects through internationally recognized qualifications.
  • Contributing to regional integration and sustainable development.

Apply for the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027 Today

If you are an ambitious student from Burundi, Kenya, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, or Uganda, this is an outstanding opportunity to pursue a fully supported undergraduate, Master’s, or PhD degree at a top university in another East African Community country.

With 197 scholarships available across 28 universities and 164 academic programmes, the EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027 is one of the region’s most competitive and impactful higher education opportunities.

Application Deadline: 20 August 2026. Don’t miss your chance to advance your education while becoming part of a stronger, more integrated East African Community.

APPLY NOW

The post EAC Student Mobility Scholarship 2026/2027: 197 Fully Funded Undergraduate, Master’s & PhD Scholarships Across East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GitLab Foundation Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026: Up to $500,000 in Flexible Grants for Workforce Development Organizations in the United States
item.dc:creator: Nana Safo
item.description:
Empowering Organizations to Create Lasting Economic Opportunities for American Workers The GitLab Foundation has officially launched the second round of its Powering Economic Opportunity Fund (PEOF), a major funding initiative designed to strengthen workforce development across the United States. Through this initiative, the Foundation is committing $4 million in flexible grant funding to support organizations […]

The post GitLab Foundation Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026: Up to $500,000 in Flexible Grants for Workforce Development Organizations in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Empowering Organizations to Create Lasting Economic Opportunities for American Workers

The GitLab Foundation has officially launched the second round of its Powering Economic Opportunity Fund (PEOF), a major funding initiative designed to strengthen workforce development across the United States. Through this initiative, the Foundation is committing $4 million in flexible grant funding to support organizations that partner directly with employers, unions, and industry leaders to improve job quality, expand work-based learning opportunities, and create sustainable career pathways for workers.

Rather than simply supporting training programs, the Powering Economic Opportunity Fund focuses on transforming employer practices that help workers remain employed, advance professionally, and achieve long-term income growth.

Organizations selected under this funding opportunity may receive grants of up to $500,000 over a period of one to two years.

About the Powering Economic Opportunity Fund

The Powering Economic Opportunity Fund is part of a broader two-year, $16 million partnership between the GitLab Foundation and the Families and Workers Fund.

The initiative was created to address one of today’s biggest workforce challenges: although thousands of workers receive skills training every year, many struggle to remain employed or advance into long-term careers. The Foundation believes that solving this challenge requires improving employer practices—not just worker training.

Round 2 of the program therefore places greater emphasis on employer-side innovations that improve employee retention, career progression, workplace quality, and long-term earning potential.

Why This Fund Matters

The United States is experiencing major economic transformation driven by several rapidly expanding sectors, including:

  • Clean and renewable energy
  • Advanced manufacturing
  • Infrastructure development
  • Skilled construction trades
  • Industrial technology

Demand for skilled professionals such as electricians, welders, energy technicians, construction managers, and industrial specialists continues to increase.

At the same time:

  • Many experienced workers are retiring.
  • Employers face growing shortages of skilled labor.
  • Artificial Intelligence is reshaping the labor market by reducing many entry-level knowledge jobs.
  • Workers increasingly need access to durable career opportunities offering stable wages and long-term growth.

The GitLab Foundation believes that investing in better employer practices can help bridge this gap and produce lasting economic benefits for workers.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Maximum grant amount: Up to $500,000
  • Funding pool: $4 million
  • Grant duration:
    • One year
    • Two years
  • Flexible funding that allows organizations to implement innovative workforce solutions.

What the Fund Supports

The Powering Economic Opportunity Fund is specifically interested in initiatives that improve long-term employment outcomes rather than only increasing training participation.

Priority will be given to projects that strengthen relationships between employers and workforce organizations while improving worker success.

Areas of interest include:

1. Employer Partnerships

Every application must include an active partnership with at least one of the following:

  • Employer
  • Labor union
  • Industry association
  • Workforce industry partner

Strong partnerships may include:

  • Hiring commitments
  • Employer-recognized credentials
  • Curriculum co-development
  • Employer co-investment
  • Apprenticeships
  • Work-based learning opportunities

Applications that do not include an active employer or industry partnership will not move beyond the concept note stage.

2. Employer Practice Change

The Foundation seeks projects that improve workplace quality through employer-led innovations.

Examples include:

  • Structured career progression pathways
  • Better supervisory and management practices
  • Employer-funded education programs
  • Predictable work schedules
  • Childcare support initiatives
  • Employee retention strategies
  • Career advancement programs
  • Workplace policies that improve long-term earnings

3. Durable Impact

Applicants should demonstrate how successful practices will continue after grant funding ends.

Possible long-term sustainability models include:

  • Employer investment
  • Collective bargaining agreements
  • Public funding
  • Other long-term financing mechanisms

The Foundation is particularly interested in projects capable of producing lasting improvements in worker earnings over time.

Expected Impact

The GitLab Foundation prioritizes initiatives that generate measurable economic outcomes.

Its goal is to create projects capable of producing at least:

  • $100 in increased worker earnings for every $1 invested by the Foundation.

This focus ensures that funded initiatives deliver meaningful and measurable economic benefits.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible applicants include:

  • U.S.-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations.
  • Organizations operating under a fiscal sponsor.
  • Nonprofit partnerships involving eligible 501(c)(3) organizations.

Organizations that are not registered as 501(c)(3) nonprofits may still apply if they partner with an eligible fiscal sponsor or nonprofit organization.

Application Process

The application process consists of multiple stages.

Step 1: Submit a Concept Note

Applicants begin by submitting a short concept note describing:

  • Their proposed initiative
  • Employer partnerships
  • Workforce impact
  • Long-term sustainability
  • Expected outcomes

Concept notes will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Step 2: Invitation to Full Application

Selected organizations will receive invitations to submit a comprehensive application during September 2026.

Step 3: Final Review

Applications will undergo detailed review through November 2026.

Step 4: Grant Awards

Successful organizations will be announced in early December 2026.

Request for Proposal or APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

Deadline to submit the concept note: August 12, 2026.

Organizations are encouraged to apply as early as possible to allow adequate time for review.

For more information and questions, visit GITLAB Foundation HERE

Or send an email to:grants@gitlabfoundation.org

Key Features of the Fund

  • Up to $500,000 in grant funding.
  • Flexible one- or two-year grant period.
  • Focus on sustainable workforce development.
  • Strong emphasis on employer partnerships.
  • Supports innovative workplace improvements.
  • Encourages work-based learning models.
  • Promotes long-term career advancement.
  • Supports higher lifetime earnings for workers.
  • Requires sustainability beyond grant funding.
  • Backed by a $16 million partnership between GitLab Foundation and the Families and Workers Fund.

About the Families and Workers Fund

The Families and Workers Fund is a collaborative funding platform supported by more than 40 philanthropic partners working together to build a stronger and more equitable economy.

The organization manages approximately $130 million in collaborative funding and is housed at the Amalgamated Foundation, an independent 501(c)(3) charitable organization.

Its partnership with the GitLab Foundation reflects a shared commitment to expanding economic opportunity and improving workforce outcomes across the United States.

Why Organizations Should Apply

Organizations working at the intersection of workforce development and employer engagement have an opportunity to secure significant flexible funding while helping shape the future of work in America.

The fund goes beyond traditional workforce grants by investing in employer practices that improve retention, advancement, and long-term earnings. Organizations with strong employer partnerships, innovative workforce models, and sustainable financing plans will be particularly well positioned to compete.

For more Grants opportunities visit OFY Website HERE

Conclusion

The GitLab Foundation’s Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026 represents an important opportunity for workforce development organizations committed to creating lasting economic change. By investing in employer partnerships, innovative workplace practices, and sustainable career pathways, the Foundation aims to ensure that workers not only gain employment but also build rewarding, long-term careers with increased earning potential.

Eligible organizations are encouraged to prepare strong employer partnerships, develop sustainable workforce strategies, and submit their concept notes before the August 12, 2026 deadline to be considered for grants of up to $500,000.

The post GitLab Foundation Powering Economic Opportunity Fund 2026: Up to $500,000 in Flexible Grants for Workforce Development Organizations in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
